name: Browser Surface
description: Rules for workflows executed in a web browser, including page context, navigation, form state, uploads, downloads, and externally visible web actions.
whenToApply: The phase happens in a browser tab or web application.
The agent must navigate pages, inspect web state, fill forms, click buttons, upload files, download files, or verify browser-visible results.
whenNotToApply: The phase happens entirely in a native desktop app.
The phase is only terminal command execution.
generationGuidance: Require the phase to confirm the active website, page, account context, and relevant page state before taking action.
Do not treat a click, form fill, or URL change as completion; require a visible page, download, upload, saved state, or submitted state to be observed.
For submit, send, publish, purchase, delete, or other externally visible actions, add an approval checkpoint before the action.
When login, permissions, human verification, or ambiguous page state blocks progress, stop and report the missing browser context.
hardRequirements: Every browser phase must include observable validation after state-changing actions.
Every external browser submission must include an approval checkpoint unless the source skill explicitly says it is draft-only or read-only.

runtimeReference.title: Browser Surface Rules
runtimeReference.purpose: Browser execution rules and common browser automation mistakes.
whenToRead: Before executing any phase that uses a browser or web application.
